claims.claim_text: Tree is a copy-on-write mutable builder. Copying a Tree shares the underlying payload until the next mutation detaches it.
Node is an owned read handle into a frozen NIF tree. Copying a Node creates another read handle to the same underlying tree snapshot.
snapshot(tree) requires a non-empty tree.
isEmpty(tree) is the guard before snapshot(tree).
createTree() starts empty output. createTree(kind; children...) and createTree(kind, info; children...) build a validated node in one call.
Constructed plugin trees are validated. createTree with kind/children validates the node shape.
withTree(kind, info) emits a balanced node: opens tag, runs body, closes tag.
kind and info tell you the raw token kind and source location on a Node.
stmtKind, exprKind, typeKind, otherKind, pragmaKind return the plugin-level node category.
inc(node) advances one token. skip(node) skips the whole current subtree.
takeTree(t, var node) copies the current subtree and advances the reader. addSubtree(t, node) copies without advancing.
add(t, childTree) appends another whole Tree.
addDotToken emits a dot placeholder. addStrLit, addIntLit, addUIntLit, addIdent, addCharLit, addFloatLit emit literal atoms.
addSymUse(symId|string, info) emits a symbol-use token.
addEmptyNode, addEmptyNode2, addEmptyNode3, addEmptyNode4 emit one to four dot placeholders.
renderTree(tree) renders raw NIF text for debugging. renderNode(node) renders the current subtree.
isValid(info) checks whether source info exists. filePath(info) returns the source path. lineCol(info) returns decoded line and column.
claims.evaluation_notes: NUANCED. The invalid `NoLineInfo` case is verified directly. A non-synthetic NIF fixture with real line info would still be useful to exercise decoded file, line, and column data.

claims.claim_text: errorTree(msg) builds a synthetic error node. errorTree(msg, at) uses at for source location. errorTree(msg, at, orig) uses at for location and orig as embedded source.
eqIdent(name) is the quick exact-name check for identifiers and symbols.
symId, symText, identText, stringValue, charLit, intValue, uintValue, floatValue read the payload of the current token.
tagId, tagText, tag are for raw tag inspection when you need exact NIF shape.
Copying a Tree does not mean shared mutation; later writes detach.
Do not snapshot an empty tree.
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. A separate helper program exits non-zero with `cannot snapshot empty Tree`.

claims.claim_text: Do not assume a copied Tree sees later mutations performed through another copy.
Do not use inc(node) as a substitute for skip(node) when the current node may have children.
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. `inc` steps token-by-token; `skip` consumes the whole subtree.

claims.claim_text: Use NoLineInfo only for genuinely synthetic structure. Preserve source info from existing nodes when output is derived from them.
claims.evaluation_notes: Mixed factual/workflow guidance. `NoLineInfo` is a real API constant, but the preserve-source-info rule needs a non-synthetic line-info fixture and is partly a design recommendation.

claims.claim_text: The ~ operator converts values to Tree fragments: Node→copy, string→strLit, NifIdent→ident, int types→intLit, float types→floatLit, bool→true/false, char→charLit, Tree→identity.
%~ parses a NIF template string with $name substitutions.
nifFragment(spec) parses a literal NIF fragment string into a Tree.
loadPluginInput() reads paramStr(1) by default and returns the input root as Node. saveTree(tree) writes to paramStr(2).
claims.evaluation_notes: NUANCED. The explicit filename round-trip is verified directly. The default `paramStr(1)`/`paramStr(2)` overloads still need an argv-driven integration check.

claims.claim_text: Node keeps the backing tree alive for you.
Emit balanced trees. Match the expected child categories for the tag you are constructing.
First step: resolve nimony executable with readlink -f $(command -v nimony), then open ../src/nimony/lib/nimonyplugins.nim relative to it.

A real end-to-end Nimony plugin is exposed through a user-facing `template ... {.plugin: "name".}` entrypoint, with the rewrite logic living in a separate plugin module that reads the input node, builds a Tree, and saves the result.
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. A temporary runtime module with `template shout* ... {.plugin: "shoutplugin".}` compiled and ran through `nimony c -r`, with the separate plugin module reading input and emitting a rewritten string literal.

claims.claim_text: The default `loadPluginInput()` and `saveTree()` overloads work end-to-end under real plugin execution driven by `nimony`.
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. The integration test exercised plugin execution through `nimony c -r` without passing explicit filenames to `loadPluginInput()` or `saveTree()`.

claims.claim_text: For new compile-time DSL rewrites in Nimony, expose a plugin-backed `template ... {.plugin: "name".}` entrypoint rather than writing a Nim macro.
claims.evaluation_notes: Design guidance. This matches the actual smartcli shape and the current Nimony plugin model, but it is a project-direction rule rather than a runtime API fact.

claims.claim_text: There are three plugin kinds: template plugin (invoked at each call site), module plugin (transforms the entire module), and type plugin (invoked for every module that uses the type).
claims.evaluation_notes: Declarative/factual knowledge about the plugin system architecture. Not a runtime API claim testable from nimonyplugins.nim alone.

claims.claim_text: Plugins are compiled with Nim 2 via `nim c -d:nimonyPlugin plugin.nim`, not with Nimony itself.
claims.evaluation_notes: Build process knowledge. Not testable from within a plugin's runtime code.

claims.claim_text: The plugin path in {.plugin: "path".} is relative to the directory of the source file containing the pragma string literal, not the call site.
claims.evaluation_notes: Compiler resolution behavior. Not testable from within a plugin's runtime code.

claims.claim_text: A template plugin input Node is wrapped in a StmtsS node containing the arguments. A typical plugin starts by checking `if n.stmtKind == StmtsS: inc n` to skip past this wrapper.
claims.evaluation_notes: Behavior of the compiler's input format. Could be tested with a nimony-driven integration test but not from the nimonyplugins API alone.

claims.claim_text: A type plugin receives two inputs: paramStr(1) is the module AST, and paramStr(3) is the type definition(s) that triggered the plugin.
claims.evaluation_notes: Type plugin protocol. The loadPluginInput overload accepting a filename is already tested in C30, but the paramStr(3) protocol requires nimony compiler integration.

claims.claim_text: Plugins are deterministic: same input produces same output. The compiler caches results and skips re-execution when possible.
claims.evaluation_notes: Compiler caching behavior, not a plugin API property.

claims.claim_text: Template plugins can be hidden inside imported modules so callers don't see the .plugin pragma.
claims.evaluation_notes: Module visibility pattern. Not a runtime API claim.

claims.claim_text: A module plugin receives the entire module after semantic analysis and must output back the complete module with transformations applied.
claims.evaluation_notes: Module plugin protocol. Requires nimony compiler integration to test.

claims.claim_text: The API validates trees constructed via createTree(kind, children), %~, and nifFragment. If a constructed tree has wrong structure, it is replaced with an ErrT node. Trees built via withTree or addParLe/addParRi are not validated.
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. createTree(CallX) with no children and nifFragment("(call)") both produce ErrT. A withTree(CallX) with no callee does NOT become ErrT.

claims.claim_text: In NIF templates, $$ produces a literal dollar sign. $name placeholders are replaced with tree fragments from the bindings.
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. nifFragment("(stmts $$)") renders with a literal $. %~ with $$name produces $ plus the substituted value.

uncovered_topics: Real source line info decoding from a non-synthetic NIF fixture
needs_stronger_tests: C17/C26 - add a fixture with real line info so filePath/lineCol and source-info preservation are tested beyond NoLineInfo

changelog.action: Refinement pass from updated plugin docs. Added C37-C46 covering plugin kinds, Nim 2 compilation, path resolution, StmtsS wrapper protocol, type plugin dual input, determinism/caching, hidden plugins, module plugin protocol, validation scope, and $$ escape. C45 and C46 are new tested claims; C37-C44 are architectural knowledge not testable from the API alone.
